sourceRef.citation: Anushri Banerji, Amol Rokde, Kajal K. Jadav et al. 2026

statement.summary: Radio collars are pivotal tools for tracking the movement, behaviour, and ecology of free-ranging wildlife. They can help understand patterns of home range, migration, habitat use, social interactions, mortality causes, and responses to environmental change. (Cagnacci et al., 2010).

proposer: Anushri Banerji, Amol Rokde, Kajal K. Jadav

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom proposes radio collars as tools for wildlife tracking with stated benefits. This is empirically validated in established literature (Cagnacci et al., 2010), but the 2026 date and 'tracing pugmarks' framing suggest novel integration with AI/ML methods not yet peer-reviewed. The core claims are sound (TRUE in isolation), but the specific technical contribution remains provisional/under validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 足跡は時間を積分して、記憶の総和となる

visualGlyph.interpretation: 首輪の電波は足跡を光の粒として追い、その軌跡が物語を編む
